title: Between Two Castles of Mad King Ludwig

metadata.notes: Content overhaul — Wingspan teaching standard. Sources: official rulebook, Stonemaier FAQ, BGG forums, community strategy guides.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Room Tiles (147 total)
Every tile is color-coded by room type, and each has its own scoring condition printed right on it:
- **Living rooms** (purple) — score based on specific room types in the 8 surrounding tiles (orthogonal + diagonal). Up to 8 VP per tile.
- **Sleeping rooms** (blue) — score 4 VP each if your castle contains at least one of every other room type; only 1 VP each if any type is missing.
- **Food rooms** (yellow) — score 2 VP per room of a specified type in specific adjacent positions (above, below, or beside as printed on the tile).
- **Outdoor rooms** (green) — score 1 VP per room of a specified type anywhere in the entire castle. Nothing may be built above an outdoor room.
- **Corridor rooms** (white) — score 1 VP per room in the 8 surrounding tiles that bears a specific wall hanging symbol shown on the tile.
- **Utility rooms** (orange) — score points for connected or chained rooms of specified types. No maximum cap — the highest-ceiling room type in the game.
- **Downstairs rooms** (black) — score 1-2 VP per room of a specified type in the vertical column directly above the downstairs room. Must be placed below the throne room row.

#### Specialty Rooms & Throne Rooms
- Specialty rooms have unique scoring printed on them — they're shuffled into the general supply.
- 3 specialty room types earned through bonuses: **Tower** (scores for rooms below it in its column), **Fountain**, and **Grand Foyer**.
- **1 throne room tile per castle** — this is your starting centerpiece. It shows 2 desired room types in specific positions, scoring 2 VP per match. On its own, it scores 0.

#### Bonus Cards
- 20 bonus cards (28 in Essential Edition) — drawn between rounds.
- Each card adds an end-game scoring condition to one of your castles (e.g., "3 VP per set of 3 different room types").
- Once assigned, bonus cards are public information — everyone can see them.

#### Other Components
- Player aids (1 per player) summarizing room types and scoring
- Score tracking pad for final scoring
- King token for determining first player and draft direction
- Rulebook with illustrated placement examples

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Core Loop
subtopics.content:
#### Semi-Cooperative Partnership Building
Here's what makes this game unique: you simultaneously build **two castles** — one shared with the player on your left, one shared with the player on your right. Your final score equals the **LOWER** of your two castles' scores. You must invest in both equally or your weak castle drags you down.

#### Game Flow
The game plays over 2 rounds of tile drafting with a bonus phase in between:

**Round 1 — Draft Room Tiles**
1. Draw a hand of **9 room tiles** from the face-down supply.
2. Simultaneously, everyone selects 2 tiles (kept secret until all players are ready).
3. Reveal your tiles. Place 1 into your left castle and 1 into your right castle, discussing placement with each partner.
4. Pass your remaining hand to the **left**.
5. Repeat for 4 total turns. After the 4th selection, 1 tile remains — discard it face-down.

**Bonus Phase (Between Rounds)**
1. Draw bonus cards (count varies by player count).
2. Assign 1 bonus card to your left castle, 1 to your right castle. Discard any extras.
3. Bonus cards add end-game scoring tied to that specific castle.

**Round 2 — Draft Again**
1. Draw a new hand of 9 tiles.
2. Same drafting process, but pass hands to the **right** (reversed direction).
3. Continue for 4 turns, discard the final remaining tile.

#### The Partnership Dynamic
When placing a tile, discuss with the partner who shares that castle. You both want it to score well, but each of you has a second castle competing for attention. Negotiation, compromise, and reading your partner's priorities are the heart of this game.

#### 3rd and 5th Tile Bonuses
When the **3rd room of any single type** is placed in a castle, a bonus triggers immediately — extra tiles, specialty rooms, bonus cards, or royal attendants depending on room type. When the **5th room of a type** is placed, you choose 1 specialty room (Tower, Fountain, or Grand Foyer) to place right away. These bonuses trigger only once per room type per castle.

#### Building Rules
- All rooms must connect orthogonally to existing tiles, building outward from the throne room.
- The castle grows on a grid — tiles go above, below, left, or right of existing tiles.
- No maximum castle size, but the tile supply limits growth.
- Once placed, tiles cannot be moved.
- **Sleeping rooms** go above the throne room row. **Downstairs rooms** go below. **Nothing** goes above outdoor rooms.

subtopics.title: Detailed Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Setup
1. Place one throne room tile between each pair of adjacent players (in a 5-player game, that's 5 castles arranged in a circle).
2. Shuffle all room tiles face-down into a supply.
3. Shuffle bonus cards into a face-down deck.
4. Give the King token to a random starting player.
5. Each player receives a player aid.

#### Drafting Procedure
- Your hand size is always **9 tiles** regardless of player count.
- Each turn: pick 2 tiles secretly, reveal simultaneously, place 1 in each castle, pass your remaining hand.
- Round 1 passes **left**. Round 2 passes **right**.
- After 4 turns, the 1 remaining tile is discarded face-down.

#### Placement Rules
- Every tile must be placed orthogonally adjacent to at least one existing tile.
- Discuss placement with your castle partner — it's a shared decision.
- **Downstairs rooms** (black) must go below the throne room row.
- **Sleeping rooms** (blue) must go above the throne room row.
- **Outdoor rooms** (green) can go anywhere, but nothing may ever be placed above them.
- Once placed, tiles cannot be moved.

#### Room Type Scoring

**Living Rooms (Purple)** — Score 1 VP per room of a specified type in the 8 surrounding tiles (orthogonal + diagonal). Maximum 8 VP per tile.

**Sleeping Rooms (Blue)** — Score **4 VP each** if the castle has at least one of each of the other 6 room types. Only **1 VP each** if any type is missing. Specialty rooms and the throne room do NOT count toward the 6-type requirement.

**Food Rooms (Yellow)** — Score 2 VP per room of a specified type in specific adjacent positions (directly above, below, or beside as indicated on the tile).

**Outdoor Rooms (Green)** — Score 1 VP per room of a specified type anywhere in the entire castle. Strong scaling, but they constrain vertical building since nothing can go above them.

**Corridor Rooms (White)** — Score 1 VP per room in the 8 surrounding tiles that bears the specific wall hanging symbol shown on the corridor tile.

**Utility Rooms (Orange)** — Score points for connected or chained rooms of specified types. No maximum cap — this is the highest-ceiling room type in the game.

**Downstairs Rooms (Black)** — Score 1 or 2 VP (as printed) per room of a specified type in the vertical column directly above the downstairs room.

#### Bonus Phase
- Draw bonus cards between Round 1 and Round 2.
- Assign 1 card to each of your castles. Discard extras.
- Cards are public — all players can see what each castle is scoring for.

#### 2-Player Variant (Ludwig)
A dummy third castle called "Ludwig" sits between the two players, creating 3 castles total. Each player shares one castle with the opponent and one with Ludwig. The adjacent human player makes all placement decisions for the Ludwig side. Your score remains the lower of your two castles.

#### Specialty Rooms
- **Fountains** are specialty rooms, NOT outdoor rooms — they lack the tree icon. Confirmed in official FAQ.
- **Towers** score for the total number of rooms below them in their column.
- **Grand Foyers** score as printed on their tile.

subtopics.title: Scoring & End Game
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
The game ends after Round 2 drafting is complete. Every game plays exactly 2 rounds plus the bonus phase — there's no variable end condition.

#### Castle Scoring
Score each castle individually by adding up:

1. **Room tile points** — evaluate every room tile's scoring condition based on its final position and adjacencies.
2. **Bonus card points** — evaluate each castle's bonus card(s) based on the castle's final state.
3. **Room type set bonus** — for each complete set of all 7 room types present in a castle, score bonus VP.
4. **Throne room points** — 2 VP per matching room in the correct adjacent position.
5. **Specialty room points** — Towers, Fountains, and Grand Foyers score according to their printed conditions.
6. **Royal Attendant points** — each scores 1 VP per wall hanging of its specified type anywhere in the castle.

#### Determining Your Score
- You contributed to exactly 2 castles.
- Your personal score equals the **LOWER** of your two castles' scores.
- A 70/50 split (score: 50) loses to a 60/60 split (score: 60). Balance is everything.

#### Winning
- Highest personal score wins.
- **Tiebreaker**: the player whose HIGHER castle scored more wins.
- If still tied: shared victory.

#### Typical Score Ranges
- Beginner games: 40-55 VP per castle
- Experienced play: 55-75 VP per castle
- Highly optimized: 75+ VP per castle

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### The Golden Rule: Balance Your Two Castles
Your score is the LOWER of your two castles. A lopsided 80/40 split gives you 40 points; a balanced 60/60 gives you 60. Never neglect either castle. If one is pulling ahead, invest your next tile in the weaker one.

#### Room Type Priorities
- **Utility rooms (orange) scale best.** A utility room scoring "1 VP per food room in this castle" grows with every food room added. Place utility rooms early and build around their condition. No scoring cap means they can be the highest-scoring individual tiles in the game.
- **Sleeping rooms (blue) are high-risk, high-reward.** At 4 VP each with all 6 other types present vs. 1 VP without, they can swing 15+ points. Commit early to collecting all room types or avoid sleeping rooms entirely.
- **Outdoor rooms (green) want edges.** They score for rooms across the entire castle (strong scaling), but nothing can be built above them. Place them at the top of columns or at castle edges.
- **Downstairs rooms (black) want tall columns.** They score for rooms in the column directly above them — not the whole castle. Place them deep below the throne room and build tall columns above.

#### Pursue 3rd-Tile Bonuses
When the 3rd room of any type is placed in a castle, a bonus triggers — extra tiles, specialty rooms, bonus cards, or royal attendants. These bonuses are often worth more than any single tile's raw scoring. Reaching 3 of a type should be a deliberate goal, not an accident.

#### Communicate With Your Partners
You share each castle with a different player. Talk about what you need. If your left partner is filling your shared castle with sleeping rooms (blue), tell your right partner you need food (yellow) and outdoor (green) rooms for the set bonus on the other castle. Collaboration beats silent play.

#### Hate-Drafting
In a drafting game, denying an opponent a key tile that completes their set or triggers a bonus can be as valuable as taking a good tile for yourself. Pay attention to what your neighbors are building.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Neglecting one castle.** Your weaker castle IS your score. Check both castles every turn.
- **Ignoring adjacency.** Many rooms score based on what's next to them. Random tile placement wastes points.
- **Hoarding one room type.** Six sleeping rooms but no food or outdoor rooms means missed set bonuses and poor diversity.
- **Burying outdoor rooms.** Nothing can go above them. Place them on top of columns, not in the middle where they block vertical growth.
- **Not reading bonus cards.** Bonus cards can be worth 10+ VP if you build around them. Assign them to the castle that best fits their condition.
- **Fighting your partner.** A slightly suboptimal placement that keeps your partner invested is better than a "perfect" placement that makes them abandon your shared castle.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of Between Two Castles

**Setup**: Each pair of adjacent players shares a castle with a throne room tile in the center. You have two castles — one to your left, one to your right. Grab your player aid and study the room type summary on it.

**Round 1, Turn 1**: Draw 9 room tiles. Look at all of them. Find 2 tiles that either score well together or start a plan — ideally one for each castle. Pick your 2 tiles and keep them secret. When everyone reveals: place 1 tile in your left castle and 1 in your right castle. Discuss with each partner where the tile should go. Remember: downstairs rooms (black) go below the throne row, sleeping rooms (blue) go above. Pass your remaining 7 tiles to the left.

**Round 1, Turn 2**: You receive 7 tiles from the player on your right. Scan for tiles that complement what you've already placed. A utility room (orange) scoring "1 VP per food room" pairs perfectly with a castle that already has food rooms (yellow). Pick 2, reveal, place, pass left.

**Round 1, Turns 3-4**: Hands shrink to 5 then 3 tiles. Fewer choices mean you need to adapt. After Turn 4, discard the 1 remaining tile. Check both castles — are they roughly balanced? Does either castle need a specific room type for the sleeping room set bonus?

**Bonus Phase**: Draw bonus cards. Read each carefully. Assign the card that best fits your left castle's existing layout to the left, and the other to the right. These are now public — your partners can see them too and should build toward them.

**Round 2**: Draw a fresh hand of 9 tiles. Same drafting process, but this time pass to the **right**. Focus on completing room type sets (for the sleeping room 4 VP bonus) and building around your bonus cards. Try to trigger 3rd-tile bonuses by reaching a 3rd room of any type.

**Scoring**: Score each castle tile by tile. Sum room tile points + bonus card points + set bonuses + throne room bonuses + specialty room points + royal attendant points. Your personal score = the LOWER castle. First game target: 50-60 per castle is solid. 65+ is excellent.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Castle Balance Is Everything
Your score equals your LOWER castle. Never let one castle fall more than 10 points behind the other. If your left castle is struggling, prioritize it even if your right castle could score more with that tile.

#### Room Synergy Chains
The highest scores come from rooms that amplify each other:
- **Utility rooms (orange) + any focused type:** A utility scoring "1 VP per food room" alongside 5 food rooms (yellow) is already 5 VP from one tile — and it keeps growing. Place utility rooms early to maximize their payoff.
- **Living rooms (purple) + dense clusters:** Living rooms score for specific types in their 8 surrounding tiles. Pack them into the center of the castle where they have the most neighbors.
- **Food rooms (yellow) + sleeping rooms (blue):** Food rooms want specific adjacencies. Sleeping rooms want type diversity. Together they naturally push you toward a balanced castle.
- **Downstairs rooms (black) + tall columns:** Downstairs rooms score for a specific type above them. Build a column of 3-4 matching rooms above a downstairs room for 6-8 VP from one tile.

#### Throne Room Awareness
Each castle's throne room scores 2 VP for matching rooms in specific adjacent positions. Identify these conditions during setup and draft rooms that satisfy them early. A fully activated throne room can be worth 8+ VP — often the difference in a close game.

#### Draft Signaling
Your drafting choices communicate intent to your partner. If you consistently pass food rooms (yellow) toward one castle, your partner should recognize you're building a food cluster. Fighting your partner's signals hurts both of you — you share the castle's score.

#### Specialty Room Timing
- **Towers** want to be placed at the top of tall columns. The more rooms below a Tower in its column, the more it scores. Time Tower placement for after you've built a 3-4 room column.
- **Fountains** are specialty rooms, NOT outdoor rooms — they don't block building above them. Place them where you'd hesitate to put an outdoor room.
- **Grand Foyers** score as printed. Evaluate them against your castle's current state when the 5th-room bonus triggers.

#### Player Count Adjustments
- **3-4 players:** Fewer castles to manage. Each castle gets more attention. Scores tend to be higher and more balanced.
- **5-7 players:** More competition in the draft. Hate-drafting becomes more important. You see fewer tiles in later picks, so early selections matter more.
- **2 players (Ludwig variant):** You control one side of Ludwig completely. Use Ludwig's castle strategically — invest enough to keep your score competitive, but don't over-invest at the expense of your shared castle with your opponent.

rulings: Fountains are specialty rooms, NOT outdoor rooms — they lack the tree icon that identifies outdoor rooms.

Sleeping rooms require at least one of each of the 6 other regular room types (food, living, utility, outdoor, corridor, downstairs) to score 4 VP. Specialty rooms and the throne room do NOT count toward this requirement.
Downstairs rooms score for the vertical column directly above the downstairs room — not the entire castle.
Nothing may ever be placed above an outdoor room tile. This is a hard placement constraint.
Each player always draws 9 tiles per round regardless of player count.
When the 3rd room of any type is placed in a castle, an immediate bonus triggers. The 5th room of a type lets players choose one specialty room (Tower, Fountain, or Grand Foyer) to place.
Bonus cards are public information — all players can see which bonuses each castle has after assignment.
In the 2-player Ludwig variant, the adjacent human player makes all placement decisions for the Ludwig side.
